role.title: Junior Advisor for the Global Gateway Investment Climate Reform Facility

compensation.display_original: Remunerated according to experience (GIZ Brussels PC200)

description_original.content:
Junior Advisor (m/f/d) for the Global Gateway Investment Climate Reform (GG ICR) Facility

Organisation: GIZ (implemented with Expertise France and SNV; posted via Euractiv Jobs)
Location: Brussels, Belgium
Engagement: Temporary contract (PC200 Belgian law)
Work arrangement: Hybrid
Source: https://jobs.euractiv.com/jobs/junior-advisor-m-f-d-for-the-global-gateway-investment-climate-reform-gg-icr-facility/

## About the programme

The Global Gateway Investment Climate Reform (ICR) Facility supports partner governments, public institutions and private-sector representatives in improving the business environment and investment climate in line with the EU’s Global Gateway strategy. Jointly co-funded by the European Union, the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), and the French Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs (MEAE), the Facility is implemented by GIZ, Expertise France and SNV.

The Facility provides high-quality technical assistance, develops and disseminates knowledge products, strengthens strategic dialogue with EU institutions and contributes to evidence-based policy processes. A central component is the ICR Knowledge Hub, which consolidates lessons learned, maintains methodological standards, and ensures cross-regional learning aligned with Global Gateway priorities.

## Tasks

Reinforcing the Brussels-based team, the Junior Advisor for the GG ICR Facility works closely with all team members, most particularly with GIZ staff but also with partner organisations across INTPA partner countries to:

- Support the implementation of the outreach strategies for the GG ICR Facility
- Support the management of technical assistance interventions in support of GG ICR partner organisations (governments, public agencies, chambers, associations etc), with a focus on business environment reform, including for each intervention:
  - Assessing the request for support from partner organisations, through interviews with requesters and other relevant actors
  - Draft Terms of Reference for the proposed intervention
  - Select and manage consultants to implement the intervention
  - Organise and hold regular exchanges with the requesters and consultants to ensure sound implementation
  - Liaise with the EU Delegations and other donors in the respective countries
  - Ensure collection of required data for monitoring and evaluating impact
  - Ensure adherence to communication and visibility rules by all actors, in particular consultants
- Contribute to GG ICR Facility knowledge management through studies, training, peer-events, especially in business environment reforms and women's economic empowerment
- Support Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) efforts, including baseline/endline data and donor reporting (EU and BMZ)

## Requirements

- Master’s degree or equivalent in economics, political science, public policy, international development, business administration, law, or another relevant field
- First relevant professional experience in international development cooperation, preferably private sector development, investment climate reform, business environment reform, public-private dialogue, women’s economic empowerment, trade, or economic governance
- First professional experience working with stakeholders of developing countries; first experience managing technical assistance interventions
- Strong conceptual and analytical skills
- Understanding of EU development cooperation, Global Gateway, EU Delegations, and Team Europe
- Excellent English; French and/or Spanish strong assets; Portuguese an advantage
- High intercultural competence, political sensitivity, flexibility
- Willingness to travel to INTPA partner countries and within Europe

## Offer

Temporary contract with GIZ in Brussels under Belgian law (PC200). Remunerated according to experience. Legal holidays plus 5 extra days, lunch checks, hospitalisation insurance, public transport ticket reimbursement, home office per policy.

Only applications for candidates eligible to work in Belgium will be accepted. Apply via the Euractiv Jobs posting Apply button.
